data.body:
### [10/10] CONVERSATION DECISIONS + RECOMMENDED PLAN + OPEN GATES + RESEARCH

USER STEERS (settled, do not re-litigate): (1) the .bridge_state files MUST stay committed in the repo — critical so the reconciler runs consistently across ephemeral CI jobs AND local environments (this killed the "make prev local-only/gitignored" option). (2) history is USEFUL for debugging but the volume is EXCESSIVE — bound it, don't eliminate. (3) user was SKEPTICAL of "prev is redundant" -> forced the adversarial proof that found prev is load-bearing (see [3]). (4) .bridge_state stays a committed on-branch merge=ours derived cache (settled by epic 6c9c; WS-1 dropped) — a separate ref revives a dropped decision.

DECISIONS MADE (via explicit choice):
- DEBUG TRAIL: keep a SEPARATE bounded full-Jira-snapshot debug artifact, MAX 1 snapshot/day, 15-DAY retention (<=15 snapshots). Decoupled from the tiny functional key-set prev.
- EXISTING ~337 MB: ONE-TIME coordinated reclaim (reconciler-paused, all-clones-converged filter-repo + lockstep enforce_since re-pin) — see [9] flag-day.
- bindings.json: INVESTIGATE NOW (done, [5]) and fold into the design.

RECOMMENDED PLAN (TABLED — implement later, in this order):
1. prev_snapshot -> KEY-SET (dict {jira_key:{}}), AFTER clearing the _emit_both open gate ([4]). Near-eliminates the 155 MB grower. Dead-code cleanup alongside: remove outbound_differ prev plumbing (OutboundDiffConfig.prev_snapshot :364, :526, :795 threading, outbound_fields.py:416 fallback) + dead seed_baselines_from_snapshot.
2. bindings.json CHURN GUARD (drop/guard baseline_advanced_at + write-when-changed in set_baseline) — biggest+safest, collapses the ~987-version-per-pass trajectory to real-change-only. THEN TEXT NORMALIZE (adf_to_text desc + status/priority .name, -66%, no migration). DIGEST is OPTIONAL and only behind a dual-write-shadow rollout; if pursued, EXCLUDE assignee (R1 killer, [7]).
3. DEBUG ARTIFACT: dated disjoint path (e.g. debug/<date>-<uuid>.json so it never collides — no merge driver needed), written <=1/day, pruned to 15 days by NOT-writing rather than history rewrite, kept OUT of fail-closed logic and ideally out of the .bridge_state commit-back or clearly scoped.
4. ONE-TIME RECLAIM: the [9] 8-step quiesced flag-day. Do LAST, after go-forward growth is fixed.

OPEN GATES (must resolve BEFORE implementing):
- G1 (blocks prev key-set): resolve the _emit_both fence ([4]) via a corpus mutation-diff (prev-full vs prev-keyset) — prove branch-3 outbound updates are dropped/superseded, or skip the both-path for empty prev, or stop feeding prev to compute_mutations. Also confirm branch-2 neutering aligns with ADR 0028.
- G2 (blocks digest, if pursued): normalization parity R1-R7 ([7]); extend the direction-preservation property suite with adversarial shape cases FIRST; assignee likely excluded.
- G3 (coordination): any prev/bindings change touches the differ/field contract shared with the ACTIVE session epic 58b0 (sync correctness) — coordinate. And each reconciler-core change should ride the proven rollout template (dual-write shadow -> N divergent=0 passes -> flag flip -> retire), the a118/d6bd playbook.
- G4 (reclaim): enforce_since lockstep re-pin + converge ALL auto-pushing clones ([9]) or the rewrite is undone by --allow-unrelated-histories.

RESEARCH / PRIOR-ART (full cited report in b96a): high-commit-volume git projects converge on moving hot/high-churn derived data OFF shared history onto HTTP/CDN (crates.io sparse index RFC 2789 + periodic squash; Homebrew 4.0 JSON-over-CDN, shallow BANNED; CocoaPods/Go-modules). git-annex `git-annex` branch + `git annex forget` = the closest analog (separate union-merged metadata branch, prune history, survives uncoordinated squash via content-convergence) — its documented failure is exactly rebar's --allow-unrelated-histories drag-back ("needs a supporting version on ALL clones"). Consumer-side (shallow/partial/commit-graph) cuts CLIENT cost only, never the authoritative store; treeless partial clone RAISES server pack CPU ~4x. VERDICT for rebar: it's a distributed union-merge store, so the on-lane fix is NOT re-homing (dropped) but SHRINKING the on-branch cache content + one-time reclaim — which is this plan.

SUBAGENT INVESTIGATIONS THIS SESSION (all cited above): repo-size measurement; prev-not-redundant red/blue team; prev consumer/field-drift sizing; clone-topology; strategy pressure-test; bindings.json sizing; + 4 history dossiers A/B/C/D. Raw reports are in the session transcript; distilled with citations in entries [2]-[9].
